concepts.name: Tawhid (divine unity)
concepts.definition: The oneness of God and what that unity rules out about His nature and acts.

concepts.name: Qadar (divine decree)
concepts.definition: Whether human actions are predetermined by God or freely chosen.

concepts.name: Kasb (acquisition of acts)
concepts.definition: Ash'ari doctrine that humans acquire acts God creates, reconciling omnipotence with human responsibility.

concepts.name: Jabr (divine compulsion)
concepts.definition: The view that humans are wholly compelled in their actions and have no real agency.

concepts.name: Sifat Allah (divine attributes)
concepts.definition: Whether God's attributes like knowledge and speech are real, metaphorical, or identical to His essence.

concepts.name: Khalq al-Qur'an (createdness of the Qur'an)
concepts.definition: Whether the Qur'an is God's uncreated eternal speech or a thing created in time.

concepts.name: Ru'yat Allah (beatific vision)
concepts.definition: Whether believers will literally see God with their eyes in the afterlife.

concepts.name: Takfir (declaring unbelief)
concepts.definition: The conditions under which a professing Muslim can be declared an unbeliever.

concepts.name: Al-Manzila bayn al-Manzilatayn (intermediate position)
concepts.definition: Mu'tazili doctrine that a grave sinner is neither a believer nor an unbeliever.

concepts.name: Husn wa Qubh (rational good and evil)
concepts.definition: Whether moral value is known by unaided reason or only by revealed command.

concepts.name: Tanzih (divine transcendence)
concepts.definition: Negating any resemblance between God and creation and stripping anthropomorphic language of literal force.

concepts.name: Tashbih (likening God to creation)
concepts.definition: Ascribing creaturely features to God, and how to read scriptural language that seems to.

concepts.name: Shafa'a (intercession)
concepts.definition: Whether prophets and saints can intercede for sinners on the Day of Judgment.

concepts.name: Fitra (innate disposition)
concepts.definition: The natural God-given orientation every human is born with toward truth.

concepts.name: Iman (definition of faith)
concepts.definition: Whether faith is inward assent, verbal profession, works, or all three combined.

concepts.name: Barzakh (the interworld)
concepts.definition: The state of souls between death and resurrection, later also used in Sufi cosmology.

concepts.name: Nubuwwa (prophethood)
concepts.definition: The nature, necessity, and proofs of prophecy and the knowledge prophets receive.

concepts.name: Isma (infallibility)
concepts.definition: Doctrine that prophets, and for Shia the Imams, are preserved from sin and major error.

concepts.name: Ghayba (occultation of the Imam)
concepts.definition: Shia doctrine that the Twelfth Imam is alive but hidden until the end of time.

concepts.name: Bada (divine alteration)
concepts.definition: Shia doctrine that God may manifest a new decree as His ongoing will unfolds.

concepts.name: Wujud and Mahiyya (existence and essence)
concepts.definition: Whether existence is really distinct from essence in created things, and what that implies for God.

concepts.name: Wajib and Mumkin (necessary and contingent being)
concepts.definition: Dividing all that exists into what must exist in itself and what might or might not.

concepts.name: Fayd (emanation)
concepts.definition: Neoplatonic scheme in which being flows from God through a hierarchy of intellects.

concepts.name: Al-Aql al-Fa'al (Active Intellect)
concepts.definition: The cosmic intellect that actualizes human thought and grants prophetic knowledge.

concepts.name: Qidam al-Alam (eternity of the world)
concepts.definition: Whether the universe had a beginning in time or is co-eternal with God.

concepts.name: Nafs Natiqa (the rational soul)
concepts.definition: The immaterial human soul in Avicennan philosophy, its faculties and survival after death.

concepts.name: Asalat al-Wujud (primacy of existence)
concepts.definition: Mulla Sadra's thesis that existence is the fundamental reality and essence is derivative.

concepts.name: Harakat Jawhariyya (substantial motion)
concepts.definition: Mulla Sadra's doctrine that substances themselves undergo continuous renewal and change.

concepts.name: Ishraq (illuminationism)
concepts.definition: Suhrawardi's philosophy of light as fundamental reality and knowledge as presence.

concepts.name: Mantiq (logic)
concepts.definition: The Aristotelian syllogistic apparatus naturalized into Islamic scholarly curricula.

concepts.name: Al-Madina al-Fadila (the virtuous city)
concepts.definition: Al-Farabi's ideal political community guided by a philosopher-prophet ruler.

concepts.name: Sa'ada (philosophical happiness)
concepts.definition: The ultimate end of human life conceived as intellectual and moral perfection.

concepts.name: Tahafut (incoherence debate)
concepts.definition: Al-Ghazali's critique of the philosophers and Ibn Rushd's reply on key metaphysical questions.

concepts.name: Alam al-Mithal (the imaginal world)
concepts.definition: An intermediate realm of subsisting forms between the material and purely intellectual.

concepts.name: Burhan (demonstration)
concepts.definition: Aristotelian demonstrative proof held up as the highest form of scientific knowledge.

concepts.name: Ijtihad (independent legal reasoning)
concepts.definition: The effort of a qualified jurist to derive rulings directly from the primary sources.

concepts.name: Taqlid (following a qualified authority)
concepts.definition: Accepting a jurist's rulings without personally deriving them from the evidence.

concepts.name: Ijma (scholarly consensus)
concepts.definition: The agreement of qualified scholars treated as a binding source of law.

concepts.name: Qiyas (analogical reasoning)
concepts.definition: Extending a textual ruling to a new case sharing its effective cause.

concepts.name: Istihsan (juristic preference)
concepts.definition: Setting aside strict analogy for a stronger contextual consideration of fairness or benefit.

concepts.name: Maslaha (public welfare)
concepts.definition: Using the common good as a criterion for legal rulings where texts are silent.

concepts.name: Maqasid al-Shari'a (higher objectives of the law)
concepts.definition: Reading shariah through its overarching aims such as protecting life, religion, and reason.

concepts.name: Urf (custom as a legal source)
concepts.definition: Local custom as a secondary source shaping how rulings apply in practice.

concepts.name: Sadd al-Dhara'i (blocking the means)
concepts.definition: Prohibiting otherwise lawful acts because they predictably lead to forbidden outcomes.

concepts.name: Naskh (abrogation)
concepts.definition: Later revelation cancelling or modifying an earlier ruling in the Qur'an or Sunnah.

concepts.name: Ikhtilaf (juristic disagreement)
concepts.definition: The legitimate diversity of rulings among qualified scholars and the schools of law.

concepts.name: Hiyal (legal stratagems)
concepts.definition: Devices that formally comply with the law while evading its underlying intent.

concepts.name: Al-Ahkam al-Khamsa (the five legal values)
concepts.definition: Classifying acts as obligatory, recommended, neutral, disliked, or forbidden.

concepts.name: Istishab (presumption of continuity)
concepts.definition: Assuming a known state of affairs remains in force until evidence changes it.

concepts.name: Marja'iyya (source of emulation)
concepts.definition: Shia institution by which lay believers follow the rulings of a senior living jurist.

concepts.name: Fana (annihilation of the self)
concepts.definition: Passing away of the ego's awareness in contemplation of God.

concepts.name: Baqa (subsistence in God)
concepts.definition: The sober return after annihilation, living in God after dying to the self.

concepts.name: Wahdat al-Wujud (unity of being)
concepts.definition: Ibn Arabi's doctrine that all existence is one reality manifesting as many.

concepts.name: Wahdat al-Shuhud (unity of witnessing)
concepts.definition: Sirhindi's alternative that oneness is in the mystic's vision, not in reality itself.

concepts.name: Tariqa (Sufi path and order)
concepts.definition: The structured spiritual path and the institutional orders that transmit it.

concepts.name: Dhikr (remembrance of God)
concepts.definition: Rhythmic invocation of God's names as the central Sufi practice.

concepts.name: Sama (spiritual audition)
concepts.definition: Controversial practice of listening to music and poetry to induce spiritual states.

concepts.name: Maqamat (stations on the path)
concepts.definition: Progressive moral and spiritual levels attained by the seeker's own effort.

concepts.name: Ahwal (spiritual states)
concepts.definition: Transient God-given conditions experienced on the path, distinct from earned stations.

concepts.name: Mahabba (divine love)
concepts.definition: Love of God as the axis of the mystical life, both human ascent and divine self-giving.

concepts.name: Ma'rifa (gnosis)
concepts.definition: Direct tasting knowledge of God beyond discursive reasoning.

concepts.name: Wilaya (sainthood and friendship with God)
concepts.definition: The station of God's friends and the hierarchy of saints, distinct from prophethood.

concepts.name: Qutb (the spiritual pole)
concepts.definition: The supreme saint of each era around whom the spiritual hierarchy turns.

concepts.name: Al-Insan al-Kamil (the Perfect Human)
concepts.definition: The cosmic archetype of humanity as complete mirror of the divine names.

concepts.name: Tajalli (divine self-disclosure)
concepts.definition: God's manifesting of Himself through created forms and inner unveilings.

concepts.name: Futuwwa (spiritual chivalry)
concepts.definition: A code of generous self-sacrificing virtue linked to craft guilds and Sufi brotherhoods.

concepts.name: Hulul (indwelling)
concepts.definition: The contested idea that the divine inhabits a human, as charged against al-Hallaj.

concepts.name: Shath (ecstatic utterance)
concepts.definition: Paradoxical, seemingly blasphemous sayings uttered in states of mystical intoxication.

concepts.name: Muraqaba (vigilant watchfulness)
concepts.definition: The contemplative practice of monitoring the heart under God's unceasing gaze.

concepts.name: Nafs (the lower self)
concepts.definition: The ego-soul whose lower impulses the seeker must discipline and transform.

concepts.name: Ilm al-Hay'a (mathematical cosmography)
concepts.definition: The science of the heavenly spheres, combining observation with geometric modeling.

concepts.name: Zij (astronomical handbook)
concepts.definition: Tables of planetary positions, star coordinates, and calendrical data for practical use.

concepts.name: Tashrih (anatomy)
concepts.definition: The study of the body's structure, where Arabic-writing physicians extended and corrected Galen.

concepts.name: Kimiya (alchemy)
concepts.definition: The tradition of transforming substances, searching for the elixir, and laboratory technique.

concepts.name: Al-Jabr (algebra)
concepts.definition: The systematic manipulation of unknowns as equations, from al-Khwarizmi onward.

concepts.name: Ilm al-Manazir (optics)
concepts.definition: The geometrical and experimental science of vision and light.

concepts.name: Akhlat (humoral theory)
concepts.definition: Galenic doctrine of four bodily humors whose balance defines health.

concepts.name: Adwa (contagion debate)
concepts.definition: Whether diseases transmit person to person, debated against a prophetic saying denying contagion.

concepts.name: Mizaj (temperament or complexion)
concepts.definition: The balance of qualities defining an individual's physical and moral constitution.

concepts.name: Hisab (arithmetic and reckoning)
concepts.definition: Calculation for commerce, inheritance, astronomy, and timekeeping.

concepts.name: Tajriba (experiment and tested experience)
concepts.definition: Deliberately controlled trial as a source of knowledge in medicine and optics.

concepts.name: Handasa (geometry)
concepts.definition: Euclidean geometry extended by Arabic-writing mathematicians, feeding astronomy and optics.

concepts.name: Khilafa (caliphate)
concepts.definition: The office of successor to the Prophet as political and religious head of the community.

concepts.name: Imama (Shia imamate)
concepts.definition: Shia doctrine that leadership of the community belongs to designated infallible Imams.

concepts.name: Wilayat al-Faqih (guardianship of the jurist)
concepts.definition: Doctrine that in the hidden Imam's absence the senior jurist holds political authority.

concepts.name: Bay'a (oath of allegiance)
concepts.definition: The formal pledge by which rulers are recognized as legitimate.

concepts.name: Shura (consultation)
concepts.definition: The duty of rulers to consult, invoked to ground both pre-modern and modern political claims.

concepts.name: Siyasa Shar'iyya (shariah-oriented governance)
concepts.definition: Discretionary rulership aimed at realizing the law's purposes beyond its textual rulings.

concepts.name: Taqiyya (precautionary concealment)
concepts.definition: Shia-associated permission to conceal one's belief under threat of harm.

concepts.name: Nass (explicit designation of the Imam)
concepts.definition: Shia doctrine that each Imam is explicitly designated by his predecessor.

concepts.name: Tafsir bi al-Ma'thur (exegesis by transmitted reports)
concepts.definition: Explaining the Qur'an mainly through prophetic reports and early authorities.

concepts.name: Tafsir bi al-Ra'y (exegesis by reasoned opinion)
concepts.definition: Explaining the Qur'an through reasoned reflection, contested when unmoored from tradition.

concepts.name: Ta'wil (allegorical interpretation)
concepts.definition: Reading beyond the apparent sense, especially for theologically ambiguous verses.

concepts.name: I'jaz al-Qur'an (inimitability of the Qur'an)
concepts.definition: The Qur'an's linguistic and rhetorical uniqueness as proof of its divine origin.

concepts.name: Asbab al-Nuzul (occasions of revelation)
concepts.definition: Historical circumstances behind specific Qur'anic passages, used to interpret them.

concepts.name: Muhkam and Mutashabih (clear and ambiguous verses)
concepts.definition: The Qur'an's own distinction between unambiguous rulings and ambiguous passages.

concepts.name: Qira'at (canonical Qur'anic readings)
concepts.definition: The canonized variant readings of the Qur'anic text transmitted from early reciters.

concepts.name: Majaz (figurative language)
concepts.definition: Whether and how non-literal speech operates in the Qur'an and prophetic reports.

concepts.name: Balagha (Arabic rhetoric)
concepts.definition: The science of Arabic eloquence developed to explain the Qur'an's literary force.

concepts.name: Ilm al-Rijal (biographical hadith evaluation)
concepts.definition: Assessing hadith transmitters' reliability to grade reports as sound, weak, or fabricated.
